name: Swedish Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat milk until warm; do not boil.
2. In a separate bowl, combine ground beef, grated onion, nutmeg, ginger, salt, and pepper.
3. Gradually add flour to the meat mixture, stirring thoroughly.
4. Mix in the warmed milk until combined.
5. Stir in the beaten eggs until the mixture is light and smooth, about 5 minutes.
6. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
7. Roll the beef into 1-inch balls.
8. Brown the meatballs under the broiler until browned on all sides; remove and set aside.
9. In a separate pan, add flour to the cooking drippings from the meatballs; stir until smooth.
10. Gradually add beef stock, stirring continuously. Bring to a boil.
11. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
12. Simmer for 5 minutes.
13. Add the browned meatballs to the sauce and simmer gently covered for an additional 15 minutes.
14. Serve in a dish.
